title: "expected initializer before ‘*’ token"
date: 2008-11-23
forum: General Help
---

### Post by redxine on 2008-11-23
I'm trying to compile something I built in FLUID. Whenever I try using g++, I get this error: test.cxx:5: error: expected initializer before ‘*’ token

Here's the code:
```
// generated by Fast Light User Interface Designer (fluid) version 1.0107

#include "test.h"

int Fl_Double_Window* int make_window() {
  Fl_Double_Window* w;
  { Fl_Double_Window* o = new Fl_Double_Window(100, 100);
    w = o;
    o->end();
  }
  return w;
}

int main(int argc, char **argv) {
  make_window();
  return Fl::run();
}

```

---

### Post by geirha on 2008-11-23
> **redxine said:**
> ```

int Fl_Double_Window* int make_window() {

```

int or Fl_Double_Window*, int or ... what should make_window() return?

---

### Post by redxine on 2008-11-23
I guess it's just an Integer. If the window is created successfully then it returns 0.

Edit:
The original .cxx file that Fluid generated did not have any return type for the function make_window(); so I just stuck 'int' in front of the whole line.

---

### Post by geirha on 2008-11-24
If you look inside the function, it returns w, which was declared as Fl_Double_Window* at the start of the function, so 
```
Fl_Double_Window* main_window() {
```
should be correct.

---
